title: Bad Bunny Wins Grammy Album of the Year, Making History for a Spanish-Language Album
text: Bad Bunny made history at the 68th Annual Grammy Awards by winning Album of the Year for Debí Tirar Más Fotos, becoming the first artist to claim this top prize with a Spanish-language album. The Puerto Rican artist defeated previous Album of the Year winner Harry Styles, who had won in 2023. Bad Bunny had previously been nominated in this category in 2024 for Un Verano Sin Ti, marking the first Spanish-language album ever nominated for the award. In his acceptance remarks, Bad Bunny referenced Puerto Rico's small geographic size, stating: Puerto Rico, believe me when I tell you that we are much bigger than 100 by 35. He expressed gratitude to the Recording Academy and those involved in creating the album. The artist then pivoted to English, dedicating the award to all the people who had to leave their homeland to follow their dreams. Throughout the evening, immigration emerged as a dominant theme, with multiple winners using their acceptance moments to criticize immigration enforcement.
